query: Google I/O 2026 keynote announcements Gemini Android cloud May 19

mako.raw:
{"query":"Google I/O 2026 keynote announcements Gemini Android cloud May 19","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1795,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://developers.googleblog.com/all-the-news-from-the-google-io-2026-developer-keynote/","title":"All the news from the Google I/O 2026 Developer keynote
            
            
            - Google Developers Blog","content":"# All the news from the Google I/O 2026 Developer keynote
This year at I/O, we announced our Gemini 3.5 series of models and upgraded Antigravity, our agent-first development platform, with new capabilities to orchestrate and build agents.
And we showed how we're making it easier than ever for agents to help you build modern and performant Android and web apps with all new tools and skills.
We also open-sourced Android skills to help LLMs execute best practices for complex workflows and APIs, like migrating to Jetpack Compose, or Jetpack Navigation 3 migration.
Following the keynotes, we’ll be livestreaming more sessions May 19-20."},{"url":"https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/google-io-2026-all-our-announcements/","title":"100 things we announced at Google I/O 2026","content":"100 things we announced at I/O 2026
Here’s a rundown of the top announcements, launches and demos at I/O 2026.
This week at Google I/O 2026, we unveiled new models, agents and tools to help you build, search, create, discover, shop and get more done.
The AI Studio Build agent can automatically generate custom images on the fly using Nano Banana.
We’re introducing Google Pics, our new image creation and editing tool, built on our latest Nano Banana model, that helps you create just about anything – from party flyers to infographics, with the creative controls you want.
Our first audio glasses, made in partnership with Gentle Monster, Warby Parker, and Samsung, will arrive this fall and will be compatible with Android and iOS devices."},{"url":"https://sheets.works/data-viz/google-io-2026","title":"Google I/O 2026 Recap: Every Announcement, by the Numbers · sheets.works","content":"May 19, 2026 — Google I/O 2026 recap, by the numbers: 100 announcements, 215 Gemini mentions, Gemini 3, Android XR glasses, and 18 of 24 predictions hit.
100 announcements, 215 Gemini mentions, and the agentic era in full sentences.
In 95 minutes at Shoreline Amphitheatre on Tuesday, Sundar Pichai said the word "Gemini" 215 times.
In 2024, that jumped to 43.
This morning, depending on how strictly you count, the live blogs filed 100 distinct announcements during Sundar's 95 minutes.
In 2023, Android led the announcement count.
Pics is a new memory layer that lives across Photos, Drive, and the Pixel camera.
The pitch is that 3.5 Flash will run inside Pixel, inside Workspace, and inside the new XR glasses, with the larger 3 model handling the harder requests in the cloud.
Tell it a problem in plain English.
Two design partners on stage: Warby Parker and Gentle Monster.
The feed merges three live blogs: Android Central, The Verge, and 9to5Google.
24 bingo squares were locked at 9 AM IST on May 19."},{"url":"https://kenhuangus.substack.com/p/google-io-2026-was-not-just-a-model","title":"Google I/O 2026 Was Not Just a Model Launch. It Was Google Showing the Agent Stack.","content":"Google’s own language around I/O 2026 is “the agentic Gemini era.” Sundar Pichai framed the keynote around Gemini products, conversational AI, infrastructure, models, and agents, not around a single isolated model release.
Google’s headline benchmarks for Gemini 3.5 Flash include Terminal-Bench 2.1 at 76.2%, GDPval-AA at 1656 Elo, MCP Atlas at 83.6%, and CharXiv Reasoning at 84.2%.
The cost caveat is important: Artificial Analysis says the model costs $1,552 to run its Intelligence Index, which is 5.5 times Gemini 3 Flash and 75% more than Gemini 3.1 Pro in that test suite.
Antigravity 2.0 is a standalone desktop application intended as a central home for agent interaction, with multiple agents, dynamic subagents, scheduled tasks, and integrations across Google AI Studio, Android, and Firebase."}]}

","score":0.8646883,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://sheets.works/data-viz/google-io-2026","title":"Google I/O 2026 Recap: Every Announcement, by the Numbers · sheets.works","content":"Google · I/O · 2026 · Recap. # Google I/O 2026 Recap. 100 announcements, 215 Gemini mentions, and the agentic era in full sentences. In 95 minutes at Shoreline Amphitheatre on Tuesday, Sundar Pichai said the word "Gemini" 215 times. He showed audio glasses, smart glasses, an agentic coding tool, a 24/7 personal assistant, and an interactive world builder. It was not a typo. The keynote pulled three of the largest live blogs in the world — Android Central, The Verge, and 9to5Google — to write down everything that hit the stage. We pulled from all three every twenty seconds, hashed each entry by headline, dropped duplicates, and bucketed every announcement into one of six categories. This is the recap, by the numbers. The headline number is 100 announcements. At I/O 2023, Google made roughly 37 announcements across the keynote.
